text.title: Continuous Discovery Agent Pipeline and Gotchas
text.summary: Defines the five weekly agent hand-offs, model assignments per agent, OST diff format rules, and LLM-specific failure modes in discovery workflows.

section.title: Weekly Agent Hand-offs
section.p: Five hand-offs per week, each gated by PM review: Mon feedback-triage (haiku) → today's opportunity-candidates from tickets/NPS/sales-calls; Tue interview-prep (sonnet) → research-questions + sampling-plan; Tue interview-coder (sonnet) → transcript to coded-quotes.json; Wed ost-synthesizer (opus) → propose OST tree-diff; Thu trio-prep (sonnet) → trio-agenda with 3 candidate opportunities; Fri weekly-readout (sonnet) → weekly-discovery.md + roadmap-input.md.

rule.statement: Use cheap models for collection and triage (haiku), sonnet for structured authoring (questions, agendas, readouts), and opus only for synthesis across transcripts and for roadmap mapping.
rule.rationale: Opus over-articulates interview questions and introduces leading prompts; opus is only needed where cross-corpus reasoning is required (synthesis, roadmap mapping).

rule.statement: OST lives at .aidocs/product_docs/discovery/ost.yaml; agents emit YAML diffs (add/update/park/archive), not full tree rewrites. PM reviews diffs before applying.
rule.rationale: Agents that rewrite the full OST instead of emitting diffs overwrite PM edits and make the version history unreadable; diff-based updates preserve the PM's own edits.

section.reference: Apply OST diffs emitted by agents — PM reviews, then runs this to apply

antipattern.description: Interview-coder paraphrasing quotes instead of copying verbatim.
antipattern.reason: Paraphrased quotes break provenance; downstream synthesis agents must consume the original span with a separate paraphrase field — reject any coded-quotes.json that lacks the verbatim field.

antipattern.description: LLM accepting "I would use X" as past-behavior evidence and coding it as an opportunity.
antipattern.reason: Future preferences are systematically optimistic; the coder must label every quote as past_behavior, preference, or speculation, and the synthesizer must filter to past_behavior only for opportunity creation.

antipattern.description: OST synthesizer emitting a full tree rewrite instead of a structured diff.
antipattern.reason: Full-tree rewrites overwrite PM edits and make diffs unusable; force JSON-patch (RFC 6902) or YAML-diff format and reject full-tree outputs.

antipattern.description: Feedback-triage agent over-weighting loudest channels (Twitter, NPS detractors).
antipattern.reason: Loudest channels are survivorship-biased toward angry users; stratify the queue by source before ranking to prevent detractor over-representation in the OST.

antipattern.description: Roadmap-mapper over-weighting last week's evidence due to recency bias.
antipattern.reason: Force rolling-90-day evidence count, not raw recency; single-week signals are noise, not trend.

section.title: Privacy Rules

rule.statement: Anonymize PII at coding time, not at synthesis time; once in the corpus, it is leaked.
rule.rationale: Redacting at synthesis time means PII was present in the interview-coder and ost-synthesizer prompts, which may be logged or cached; redact the source transcript before any LLM call.

rule.statement: Do not let agents auto-book interviews into deep-work blocks; require a "discovery zone" calendar block as the only legal slot for agent-scheduled interviews.
rule.rationale: Agents scheduling into deep-work blocks corrode the PM's ability to do the synthesis work that makes the weekly cadence valuable; the calendar zone constraint is the only reliable protection.

section.reference: Friday readout skeleton: outcome, touchpoints, OST diff, roadmap input
